subject: FW: To Lesley re: Nov 2-5
body:
From: Tramo, Mark Jude [mailto:mark_tramo@hms.harvard.edu] 
Sent: Tuesday, October 23, 2007 9:01 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: To Lesley re: Nov 2-5

 



As 10/31 I will enter a "think-tank" in Cold Spring Harbor (7
neuroscientists and 7 humanists living together for 3 days to tackle the
question of Music, the Arts, and Neuroscience  (Maybe something magical
will happen!)

I'd love to drop by and tell Jeffrey about what Changeux, McCleland, I
and the group came up with, plus bring him up to date on Institute
activities - what we did last year, what we aim to do next.

Also - I sent an email last week w/ info about a tech grant to develop a
patent for a "Soundcubator" for the little premies in the Neonatal ICUs
all over the world who hear little except alarms, beepers, and
themselves crying - but never again mom's voice or other natural sounds
once their removed form the womb out of medical necessity.  is it
surprising so many end up w/' speech and language impairments throughout
their lifetime? That's one of the business-related items I'd like to
discuss w/ JE. 

Perhaps breakfast or lunch on Mon 11/5? I'll also be in town Friday
evening and all day Saturday & Sunday.

Best,
Mark
mtramo@hms.harvard.edu
body_html:
From: Tramo, Mark Jude [mailto:mark_tramo@hms.harvard.edu]
Sent: Tuesday, October 23, 2007 9:01 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: To Lesley re: Nov 2-5

 



As 10/31 I will enter a "think-tank" in Cold Spring Harbor (7 neuroscientists and 7 humanists living together for 3 days to tackle the question of Music, the Arts, and Neuroscience  (Maybe something magical will happen!)

I'd love to drop by and tell Jeffrey about what Changeux, McCleland, I and the group came up with, plus bring him up to date on Institute activities - what we did last year, what we aim to do next.

Also - I sent an email last week w/ info about a tech grant to develop a patent for a "Soundcubator" for the little premies in the Neonatal ICUs all over the world who hear little except alarms, beepers, and themselves crying - but never again mom's voice or other natural sounds once their removed form the womb out of medical necessity.  is it surprising so many end up w/' speech and language impairments throughout their lifetime? That's one of the business-related items I'd like to discuss w/ JE. 

Perhaps breakfast or lunch on Mon 11/5? I'll also be in town Friday evening and all day Saturday & Sunday.

Best,
Mark
mtramo@hms.harvard.edu
